tweets.text:
Diagram of the LLM Knowledge Base system.

Feed this to your favorite agent and get your own LLM knowledge base going. https://t.co/nPSNi4Ayqv
Interesting take, consistent with the surprising @METR_Evals study that showed coders using AI tools took a hit on productivity even they imagined otherwise.

Tech spend is going to AI

More charts: https://t.co/tusDMkBz8W https://t.co/K1zWLNV5d4

When your voice agent debugs your slides live

@charlierguo is using gpt-realtime-1.5 https://t.co/W2vE4IgYLK

The RAG era was short-lived, but intense. 

(Not that RAG is not useful, but it is no longer the dominant paradigm for supplying context to agents)

Microsoft 365 connectors are now available on every Claude plan.

Connect Outlook, OneDrive, and SharePoint to bring your email, docs, and files into the conversation.

Get started here: https://t.co/EdoQeT8BBN https://t.co/sOrigP41FJ

To celebrate the Gemma 4 launch we held a small impromptu get together with @yagilb from @lmstudio fame

Feat. @victormustar from @huggingface 

Go Local AI!! ❤️❤️ https://t.co/nAgOpdptWk
GEN-1 robotics model really seems like a major breakthrough! This could be the ChatGPT moment of robotics and open the path toward physical general intelligence (PGI) for robotics!
Am sure glad they solved the hallucination problem! https://t.co/c1e6F7BDDR

The boys are back! Burke, James and Pierce are live in 10 minutes with a walkthrough of the latest enhancements to VS Code's Agent Host Protocol! This one is perfect for developers building agent-powered VS Code extensions and automation workflows. ➡️ https://t.co/Z5YhTh5LUu https://t.co/5EKluYxyGn

This is a fascinating Rorschach test.

Codex better at backend, security, many general coding tasks.

Claude better at project management, communication.

You can tell how someone thinks, what they’re working on, and what their values are by what they prefer.
I keep hitting quota limits from GitHub's API. This hasn't been designed with agents in mind.

Is this a system prompt or a user prompt? I would not be relying on a user level prompt for protecting against abuse.

I notice there is an instruction to Claude in the comment. It seems risky to teach Claude to follow instructions it finds in source code comments.
Gemini has been posting its solutions directly to https://t.co/fqfl9BoXzj. Everyone is still in the "open due to lack of attention" stage of math discovery.

We also open sourced the code for this app, so you can build on top of it. Enjoy!

https://t.co/itpa4NVd2j

llama.cpp and gemma 4 on a 6 year old Macbook Pro M1 Max https://t.co/uPbU90GW2p
If it’s not either pushing the frontier meaningfully or open-source, no one will care these days (which is why most orgs should release open-source to get some attention and developer mindshare)
